rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JACK MICHAEL SARGENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/21 for an appearance. (No more hearing spots available.) Issues requiring appearance: There is no authority for an attorney to receive payment for services in advance of an order authorizing the payment. [CRC 7.755.] This includes fees paid personally by a fiduciary who then seeks to be reimbursed. Notice was not given, pursuant to Probate Code Section 851, to the bank from which the co-conservators seek to transfer funds. (In fact, no proper notice was given to any of the interested parties using the mandatory JC form GC-020, but waivers of notice by some of the parties using a Civil form were filed.) Other issues: It is unclear what the settlement agreement dated 11/25/19 is; none appears to have been filed. No receipts for the distributions have been filed. The confidential file had not yet been delivered to the assigned Examiner, so original bank statements have not yet been reviewed. (The first page of the pleading does not show the related case. [LRSF 14.48 B.3.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUADALUPE OCHOA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/21. The Court will not review three accountings in one petition. The administrators should have been accounting all along as the estate administration was delayed. [LRSF 14.59.] If waivers of accounting cannot be filed, an amendment or fully amended petition must be filed in order to seek approval of the first two-year accounting first. The petition caption fails to disclose all relief sought, as the administrators seek fees for extraordinary legal services. [CRC 7.102.] Distribution cannot be to an estate. The personal representative or trustee or other successor(s)-in-interest of the post-deceased heir must be set forth and must receive notice. [LRSF 14.67 I.] If the attorney is retaining the original financial statements, she must verify the petition as to that allegation and must also include all of the statements required by LRSF 2.11 M.4, which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." If the original(s) will be retained by one of the fiduciaries, the fiduciary must declare that she or he will retain the original(s) for the required period. Notice was mailed to Victor Manuel Valencia Vargas at an address that differs from the one set forth in the petition. The verification of the proof of service was not quite correct, as there is a place for the date of the verification and a place for the name to be written or typed. (It would also be helpful if the names on the service list were in the same order as in the petition.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner before 2/26/21.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BILLIE T. SCOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/2021 1) Please explain on Schedule C entry 04/30/2019 Witherspoon & Siracusa / Cost advance reimb Court order 2/7/201 for $759.05. Unclear what order this is referring to as the year has been cut off. 2) Conservator time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. [LRSF 14.53]. 3) On the conservator timesheets please explain in detail entry 4/23/2020 Review of invoice(s) / preparation of payment(s) for 10 hours as 10 hours appears excessive. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/16/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE CHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No proof of service is on file. 2) The date of the filing of the petition stated in paragraph I appears incorrect. 3) The date of issuance of letters stated in paragraph I appears incorrect. 4) The restatement date of the devisee trust stated in paragraph II appears incorrect. 5) There is no allegation as to whether notice was given to any public entity under Probate Code sections 9201 and 9202 and whether claims have been filed by any creditor. 6) The last names of Benjamin and Nikolaj appear to be misspelled in paragraph V. Also, has Nikolaj's address changed since the filing of the previous proof of service of notice? 7) Is the petitioner the only person having legal custody of and residing with Alyson Chang? 8) Please provide a copy of the Hawaii court order authorizing direct distribution of the net proceeds from the sale of the condominium to the devisee trust.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEIR O'BRIEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/2021. The 01/27/2021 Declaration does not adequately address examiner notes. 1) The court requires verification of the ending balance of cash. The ending balance of assetsin interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 2) Please explain the 04/24/2019 invoice of $65.00 paid to attorney Nathan Pastor. (CRC 7.700). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF FUMI MATSUOKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/01/2021. 1) No proof of payment of assessment has been filed. 2) Allegations required by Probate Code section 1064(a) are missing. 3) The summary of account must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form (GC-400 (SUM)). CRC 7.575(e)(5). 4) An original statement from Allstate Life Insurance Company verifying the ending market value must be filed. 5) Please explain why two separate hourly rates were applied to the services provided by the same paralegal. 6) The trust from which the petitioner and attorneys seek payment of their fees is not under the Court's supervision. Does Probate Code section 2646 allow the Court to determine the fees and "direct" the trustee of the conservatee's unsupervised trust to pay?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: OBJECTIONS TO INVENTORY AND APPRAISAL
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although no courtesy copy of the Objections to Inventory and Appraisal has been received yet by the assigned Examiner. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although the second amended petition has numerous issues, including being incomplete (no accounting attached) and still showing the same summary of account as in the amended petition, among other things. Original bank statements have not yet been filed in the confidential file. (A preliminary tentative ruling posted on 1/19/21 directed Counsel to provide a SASE so that some original statements that were sent to the Examiner could be returned to Counsel for filing; papers should not be sent to an Examiner, unfiled.) As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY CORBETT RICHMOND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/2021. 1) Has not filed DE-147S. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA VALUROSO SAILOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No proof of publication is on file. 2) Please file a verified supplement providing the following: A) The date of death of the predeceased spouse; B) The names of the decedent's parents and dates of their death; and C) The names and dates of death of the decedent's predeceased siblings without issue. 3) A confidential supplement (DE-147S form) must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet. LRSF 14.18(E) & 14.60(E)(8) (DE-147S form cannot be e-filed). 4) Please also explain why San Francisco is the proper venue for this probate, even though the decedent was a resident of San Mateo County. Probate Code section 7051.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLENN PATRICK VALUROSO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No proof of publication is on file. 2) Please file a verified supplement addressing the following issues: A) Item 8 of the petition alleges that the decedent's son's address is unknown, but the proof of service of notice shows his address in Arizona. B) No declination to act or consent to the petitioner's appointment signed by the other two children of the decedent having equal priority has been filed. LRSF 14.24. 3) Item 2 of the DE-147 form must provide the petitioner's home address (P.O. Box address is not allowed). 4) A confidential supplement (DE-147S form) must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet. LRSF 14.18(E) & 14.60(E)(8) (DE-147S form cannot be e-filed). 5) Please explain why San Francisco is the proper venue for this probate, even though the decedent was a resident of Arizona. If the decedent was a nondomiciliary of California, but left an estate in San Francisco, item 3a(2) must be completed.
